proposals.title: Persistent Issue: Bullish 'Leg Agreement' Factor Contradicts Active Guidance for PUT and Bearish Narrative
proposals.why: The structured Intent engine incorrectly cites 'Current leg agrees' at tick 21. This bullish factor directly contradicts recent Playbook guidance for 'GUIDE_PUT' at 9:48 AM and AI prose detailing momentum stalling. This mismatch confirms persistent logical disjointedness between structured factors and current mindset, as previously reported in this session.
proposals.proposedAction: Investigate incoherence between the structured Intent engine's 'Current leg agrees' factor and the opposing PUT direction guided by the Mindset engine.

proposals.title: Persistent Contradiction: Intent Engine Cites 'Leg Agreement' Despite Negative Price Action
proposals.why: The structured Intent Engine asserts 'Current leg agrees' to support a bullish CALL intent at tick 41, directly contradicting dynamic transmission data which show negative market moves. This confirms the persistent logic failure reported in previous snapshots (ticks 21, etc.) where structured criteria are not cohesive with real-time price action.
proposals.proposedAction: Fix the logical linkage in the Intent Engine so that 'Current leg agrees' only appears when immediate market movement aligns with the intent direction.

proposals.title: Structured Intent Logic Contradiction: Price Persistence Gap Mismatch
proposals.why: Structured intent data displays logical inconsistency. While 'Price persistence' is correctly listed as a 'Gap' preventing execution (given the negative immediate market move), the factor generation logic remains unstable, contradicting earlier session stability and creating disjointed internal state reasoning.
proposals.proposedAction: Evaluate the logic used to generate supporting factors and execution gaps. The simultaneous listing of 'Price persistence' as a gap while actual price movement is negative suggests the structured condition logic is not cohesive with the immediate market data, and prior fix verifications on this factor are unstable.

proposals.title: Output Defect: Duplicate Entries in Intent Gaps Array
proposals.why: The structured 'gaps' list within the intent data contains duplicate entries. While functionally blocking entry as intended, duplicate items indicate sloppiness in state data generation logic, reproducing instability themes reported previously in this build.
proposals.proposedAction: Inspect the function responsible for populating the 'gaps' array in the structured intent response to ensure distinct items are added. Deduplicate the list before outputting.

proposals.title: Persistent Contradiction: Intent Engine Claims 'Leg Agreement' During Active Negative Price Action
proposals.why: Structured logic disjointedness has regressed. The 'Current leg agrees' supporting factor is incorrectly active during substantive negative price movement (SPX -1.76, SPY -0.09), directly contradicting real-time data and reproducing logic breakdowns previously seen.
proposals.proposedAction: Investigate logical linkage between real-time transmission data (price velocity) and the structured 'Current leg agrees' supporting factor. The factor is activating despite counter-directional flow, indicating unstable fix verification and persistent regression.

proposals.title: Persistent Slackness: Duplicate Entries Continue in Intent Gaps Array
proposals.why: The structured 'gaps' list within the intent data persistently contains duplicate entries, repeating the defect identified at tick 248. While functionally appropriate for blocking entry, the duplication indicates unmitigated sloppiness in the state data generation logic.
proposals.proposedAction: Implement distinct item logic or use a data structure that enforces uniqueness (e.g., a set) when populating the 'gaps' array in the Intent model to fix sloppiness in state generation.

proposals.title: Persistent Contradiction: Intent Engine Still Claims 'Leg Agreement' During Substantive Negative Price Action
proposals.why: Structured logic disjointedness persists. The 'Current leg agrees' supporting factor is incorrectly active during substantive negative price movement (SPX -1.23, SPY -0.09), directly contradicting real-time data and repeating logic breakdowns previously seen.
proposals.proposedAction: Investigate logical linkage between transmission data (price velocity) and the structured 'Current leg agrees' supporting factor. The factor is activating despite counter-directional flow, indicating persistent regression.

proposals.title: AI Narrative Describes High Conviction Logic Override, Diagnostics Fails to Update Reentry State
proposals.why: The AI journal explicitly details material new evidence and a 'high-conviction setup forming' for a BUY_CALL, yet the structured reentry diagnostics remain blocked by 'repeated GEX_VELOCITY' with no stated evidence. This indicates a critical breakdown and regression where the model fails to apply its logical override criteria based on recognized narrative, repeating failures reported in previous build sequences.
proposals.proposedAction: Investigate failure of AI prose analysis to logically map described high-conviction momentum evidence into structured reentry diagnostics.

proposals.title: Intent Engine Reports 'Current Leg Agrees' Despite AI Narrative Detailing Stasis and Lack of Momentum
proposals.why: The intent engine's structured diagnostic cites 'Current leg agrees' as a supporting factor for PREPARE_CALL, directly contradicting simultaneous AI journal prose which describes a continuation of 'quiet accumulation' with 'localized tension easing' and 'immediate breakout momentum...not yet re-activated'. This demonstrates incoherence between the narrative and structured intent criteria.
proposals.proposedAction: Trace the logic mapping AI prose descriptions of 'stasis' or 'extended accumulation' to internal 'leg agreement' factors. The intent engine should not claim leg agreement to support a Prepare action when the narrative explicitly details a lack of dynamic momentum or new evidence.

proposals.title: Intent Engine Claims 'Leg Agreement' While AI Narrative Details Violent Counter-Directional Compression
proposals.why: The intent engine continues to report 'Current leg agrees' as a supporting factor for PREPARE_CALL, despite simultaneous AI journal prose detailing violent negative compression builds (-0.59). This confirms persistence of the logic breakdown where structured criteria become disjointed from the recognized market narrative.
proposals.proposedAction: Trace logic mapping AI prose descriptions of violent compression/negative tension to internal 'leg agreement' factors. The intent engine should not claim leg agreement to support a Prepare action when the narrative explicitly details counter-directional local pressure build.

proposals.title: Intent Engine Claims 'Leg Agreement' While AI Narrative Describes Stationary Price and Normalizing Negative Tension

The intent engine incorrectly claims immediate bullish leg agreement when narrative evidence indicates stasis and pressure builds.

proposals.title: Intent Engine Asserts 'Leg Agreement' Contradicting AI Narrative of Local Drifting

proposals.title: Structured Intent Criteria Contain Logical Contradiction Between Supporting Factors and Gaps

Furthermore, previous bug reports (ticks 161, 181, 201) regarding 'Current leg agrees' persist, indicating disjointed structured logic generation. Therefore, the highest value observation is the internal contradiction in data structure between supporting factors and gaps definitions.
proposals.proposedAction: Investigate the logic that defines structured supporting factors and gaps to ensure a condition cannot simultaneously exist in both lists.
